top_profane.text:
I use a vintage Montgomery Ward 2HP Portable Air Compressor Model XER-6409 that was given to me by my late father. Despite its age, that compressor still remains in excellent overall working condition but the original 1/4”-18 drain cock could no longer seal tightly enough to prevent air leakage. The resulting gradual pressure loss was quite annoying.

The original parts list for my compressor provided a part number for a replacement 1/4”-18 drain cock. However, an online search for that part’s current availability revealed that it’s a Campbell Hausfeld Drain Cock 1/4"-18 ST030100AV which is no longer available for purchase… from anywhere.

The 1/4”-18 NPT drain cock specs are fairly commonplace, so I next decided to conduct a search of my local hardware stores to see if I could possibly find a suitable replacement in-store option. Doing so yielded pretty unimpressive results. For the most part, each store I visited offered only one drain cock matching those specs and the limited offerings I did find were either of disappointing quality and/or were of the thumb screw type having a knurled edge. Personally, I do not find those thumb screws to be as “easy to grip” for opening/closing as the manufacturers like to claim. Instead, I much prefer the wing nut style that offers better leverage for easier opening and closure, just like the original one on my compressor.

Given those lackluster local findings, I was pleased to find that Milton offers this particular drain cock. I’ve previously purchased Milton pneumatic products and find them to be some of the best. I also like that they offer Made in USA quality.

I was very pleased to find that this Milton drain cock is essentially identical to my compressor’s original drain cock. Both my original drain cock and this Milton one are constructed of a brass body with steel handle. Handle dimensions are also equivalent at 1-3/8” long by 3/8” wide (wing width). It fits my compressor perfectly and seals tightly!

One attribute I particularly like about this Milton drain cock is that it does not depend upon a rubber O-ring that could wear out and leak air. Instead, it seals well with two machined brass mating surfaces that should never wear out.

In summary, I’m really pleased with this Milton drain cock. The fact that it comes in a 2-pack makes it even a better value, especially when you consider its overall quality combined with the relatively low cost. I’ll definitely be purchasing this again… if and when it’s ever needed.
top_profane.title: Fits perfectly and works great on my decades-old air compressor! 'Made in USA' quality.

I read so many reviews before I purchased this disposal as I haven't had one in quite a while. It is quieter than I expected as some reviews said it was a little loud. My husband installed this in less than 30 minutes (didn't need a car jack either). We have a septic so I also checked to make sure it would be a good fit. I went with the 1 horse because I felt it would grind better and be easier on the septic. The list of what to put in it wasn't very specific so I did more research and found the following:
Garbage Disposal Do’s:
• Do keep your garbage disposal clean. Pour a little dish soap inside and let the garbage disposal run for a minute or so with some cold water after washing dishes.
• Do run your garbage disposal regularly. Frequent use prevents rust and corrosion, assures that all parts stay moving and prevents obstructions from accumulating.
• Do grind food waste w/ a strong flow of cold water. Why cold water? It will cause any grease or oils that may get into the unit to solidify, so that they can be chopped up before reaching the trap.
• Do grind certain hard materials such as small chicken and fish bones, egg shells, small fruit pits, etc. A scouring action is created by these particles inside the grind chamber that cleans the garbage disposal’s walls.
• Do grind peelings from citrus fruits such as lemons or oranges to freshen up drain smells.
• Do cut large items into smaller pieces. Put them into the garbage disposal one at a time instead of trying to shove a large amount in at once.
Garbage Disposal Don’ts:
The most important rule of thumb: Don’t Put Anything In The Garbage Disposal That Is Not Biodegradable Food. A garbage disposal is not a trash can; it’s for food scraps only. Non-food items can damage both blades and the motor. When in doubt, throw it out!
• Don’t grind glass, plastic, metal or even paper.
• Don’t grind anything combustible.
• Don’t grind cigarette butts
• Don’t pour grease, oil or fat into your garbage disposal or drain. Grease will slowly accumulate and impede your garbage disposal’s grinding ability as well as clog drains.
• Don’t use hot water when grinding food waste. Hot water will cause grease to liquefy and accumulate, causing drains to clog.
• Don’t grind extremely fibrous material like corn husks, celery stalks, onion skins, and artichokes. Fibers from these can tangle and jam the garbage disposal motor and block drains .
• Don’t turn off the motor or water until grinding is completed. When grinding is complete, turn off the garbage disposal first. Let water continue to run for at least 15 seconds, flushing out any remaining particles. Then turn off water.
• Don’t put too many potato peels down the garbage disposal. The starches in the potatoes will turn into a thick paste and may cause blades to stick.
• Don’t put large amounts of food down the garbage disposal. Feed food into the garbage disposal a little at a time with the cold water running; this will help the food scraps flow down freely through the drain pipes and plumbing.
• Don’t put expandable foods into your garbage disposal. Foods like pasta and rice expand when you add water in a pot; they do the same thing once inside your pipes or garbage disposal and are the cause of many jams and clogs.
• Don’t grind large animal bones (beef, pork etc.).
• Avoid putting coffee grounds down the garbage disposal. They won’t harm the garbage disposal and they’ll actually help eliminate odors. However, they can accumulate in drains and pipes, causing clogs. Best to avoid.
• Don’t use harsh chemicals like bleach or drain cleaners. They can damage blades and pipes. Borax is a natural sink cleaner and sanitizer that effectively works on odor-causing mold and mildew that accumulates in garbage disposals. (See more below).
top_profane.title: Very Happy With This Garbage Disposal

top_profane.text: So I'm going to tell you right away, I didn't buy the flex paste from Amazon. I bought it from Menards, but I figured since I'm a prime member and a lot of people read reviews on here this would be the best place. 1st. This stuff isn't exactly cheap, I think I paid like $30 for 2 lbs at Menards for this crap and that's exactly what it is is CRAP! In the company's wonderful tv ads, it shows the guy spreading this stuff over flowing water and sealing.... That's a LIE! Due to it being rainy season, water is trickling into my basement in the corner so I figured I would try this stuff and seal it. As you can see in the pictures, it would not stick to the wall where it was wet at all, hence all of it that fell to the ground is wasted. Mind you, this is just a slight trickle so there is no way in hell it could seal gushing water! 2nd, I bought this crap about a year ago and stored it away in a  closet until today. I NEVER broke the seal on the container and yet when I opened it the top 2/3 was already hardened and only got to try 1/3 of it! The container wasn't damaged either. 3rd. Don't waste your money on this crap! This is just another crap product that a scam artist company sells you and tells you it works, but it doesn't. It's already way too overpriced and yet you can get a 5 gallon pail of roofing tar for cheaper than 1lb of this garbage, that would probably seal better than this stuff. And 4th, this stuff is extremely messy and thick. If you choose to use gloves, make sure they're cheap disposable ones. If you, don't wear gloves and get it on your hands, you'll have to wait until it dries before peeling it off.

top_rant.text:
First of all I want to say that I am a around the house handy man and not a pro but I know quality when I see it and I have had a very decent amount of experience with power tools.

I am guilty of owning a Craftsmen 3/8 inch, 1/3 horse power, 3 amp, corded drill "I got it because it was on sale for close to half price,I paid $20 for it.

When ever I tried to use it as a screwdriver with its so called variable speed trigger,it would almost always rotate to fast which would result in it stripping the screw heads.

Most times when I tried to use it for tightening nuts or drilling metal it would stall,so to make a long story short it was under powered and the variable speed control was almost uncontrollable !! Oh well you live and you learn !!!

So it was time for me to get a "high quality drill".

I now wanted a drill that would have plenty of power and have a "very controllable" variable control trigger and most of all I wanted this drill to be able to last me the rest of my life and I wanted it to be the best darn drill money could buy and at a reasonable price,now is that asking for to much ?

So I went on the net and started reading a ton of reviews,I read up on cordless drills first but I was turned off by their overall price along with the "over priced batteries they use",and lets also remember their not the most powerful drills you can buy,they simply cannot supply you with the torque a corded drill can,and when you push a cordless drill it puts a tremendous strain on the battery and in return the same battery that might have held its charge for hours using it for light duty will now lose it's charge in a matter of minutes when you use it for heavy duty use.

I have heard of some of the best batteries losing their charge within 3 minutes after using the cordless drill to do a really tough job.

I still think cordless would be nice for light duty jobs as a second drill if you can afford one!

So after reading mega reviews on the net, I was still having a little trouble trying to narrow it down to one drill,but the Milwaukee 0300-20 and the "Milwaukee 0302-20 a keyless version of the 0300-20" caught my eye with it's 8 amp motor and with the claim that it has the best power-to-weight ratio in its class.

All I could think was yum yum,MASSIVE POWER and TORQUE galore, and lets not forget that it's made by Milwaukee!!!!

Now I'm thinking I've got to have this drill no matter what, so I called a local tool store and asked if they had the 0300-20 and they said yes,so out of curiosity I asked them if they felt that the 0300-20 was the best in it's class and to my surprise he burst my bubble and told me no, and he said that the

Milwaukee 0234-6 Was The Best In It's Class !!!

Man I was so surprised at what he had said,and the man that I talked to was the owner of that shop, so I'm thinking to myself he must not know what he's talking about,I knew a little something about the 0234-6, I knew that it had a 5.5 amp motor and that it was a very old model, and I also knew that the 0300-20 was a much newer model with an "8 amp motor" and they cost around the same,so I'm thinking to myself "Duh" that sounds like a no brainer to me...old vs new, 5.5 amps vs 8 amps,come on, give me a break!

I was also thinking to myself do the math Mr. Tool shop owner! So I got off of the phone with him and called another local tool shop and talked to the boss who's name was Mike and asked him the same set of questions and low and behold he gave me pretty much the same answer which was the 0234-6 is a much better drill then the 0300-20,then Mike went on and told me that the 0234-6 is the best drill on earth bar none !!!

He said that he has had his 0234-6 for 15 years and he used it as a pro and in 15 years it never failed him once,and it never needed any repairs !

Now I'm thinking I must be in the Twilight Zone and I'm also thinking does Mike not know that the 0300-20 has 8 amps and the 0234-6 only has 5.5 amps and the 0300-20 is a much newer model.

I need to also tell you that some people think the 0300-20 and the 0302-20 are just like the 0234-6 but only with more power......WRONG !!!!

I even called Milwaukee tools and talked to a rep and he told me the 0300-20 was just an updated and more powerful version of the 0234-6...."I found out later that the rep Was WRONG"!!!

After thinking for awhile I decided to get the 0300-20 over the Keyless 0302-20 for these reasons...

Keyed vs. Keyless Chucks

Keyed chucks are tightened and loosened with a small hand tool, achieving a tighter and more secure lock on the drill bit than keyless chucks. They also provide greater torque for when you need extra power. On the other hand, keyless chucks require you to manually turn the chuck, making for quick and easy bit changes.

* Keyed chucks are especially advantageous when removing screws

* For those who may be prone to losing a key, a keyless chuck would be a sound selection !

So today I went to Lowes and had already decided in my mind that I was going to buy the more powerful 0300-20, which is supposed to be "the so called newer version of the 0234-6".

When I got to Lowes, their they both where side by side the 0234-6 and the all powerful 8 amp 0300-20,but wait just a minute the 0300-20 does not have the same rock solid sturdy side handle that is screwed into the metal frame of the 0234-6, so when I held the 0300-20 in my hands and put a lot of pressure on the side handle it flexed and I said to myself "Oh No" I don't like this handle and most of all I hate the way that it is attached to the drill,it is attached by a metal band instead of being screwed into the metal frame,like they did with the 0234-6!I think the handle on the 0300-20 is attached in a very cheap,tackie and not very strong manner!!!

So the side handle on the 0300-20 was almost a deal breaker for me,I could not break the handle ,but I sure did make it flex more then I think it should have!

Now on the other hand I tried to make the 0234-6 side handle flex or bend, no such luck it was rock steady , and the entire 0234-6 is built like a tank and I'm not kidding.

Next I tried out the trigger on the 0300-20 and the action of the trigger was not smooth at all, it was very choppy at best,let me tell you their is no way on earth for you to use a drill as a screwdriver if you don't have smooth trigger action.

Plus the trigger was a short one or two finger trigger,unlike the long and Luxurious trigger on the 0234-6!!!

I will go one step further and say I feel that it is potentially dangerous to try and use a super powerful drill when its side handle is not very steady and when its variable speed control trigger is choppy.

You cannot control the speed with a choppy trigger...so that was the deal breaker for me, a bad handle and a choppy trigger !!!!

Next I tried the trigger action on the 0234-6 and it was super smooth, I was truly impressed with the 0234-6 and very disappointed with the 0300-20.

At this point it was obvious to me that Milwaukee Cut Some Corners When They Made The 0300-20 and the 0302-20,and here's 3 prime examples !!!!!

1.The poorly attached handle

2.The short and "choppy action" trigger

3.Plus,It does not have the Removable brush cartridge,like the 0234-6 has!!!!

How else do you think Milwaukee could sell an 8 amp drill for the same price of a 5.5 amp !!!!

Do the math please,and think about which one you want quality or

quantity!!!!

Well I chose quality over quantity so needless to say I took the 0234-6 home with me.

When I tried it out, I was so thrilled and shocked by the super high quality of this work of art !!! The speed control when using the trigger was superb ,you can use this drill as a super screwdriver any day of the week, I could get it to spin at the slowest speed imaginable,and when I pulled the trigger in all the way this high quality drill spun with such smoothness and power that I found myself laughing out loud and thinking to myself this drill is a master piece.

Speaking of power, the 0234-6 has a whole ton of power and I can't ever imagine it stalling on me or anyone else for that matter"Just read all the write ups on it"!!!

Don't be fooled by the other drills that have 8 and 9 amps, to many of the high amp monsters have a short life span and none of them not a single one of them can hang with the 0234-6 as far as quality and durability is concerned.

So as far as the 0300-20 vs 0234-6 goes

their really is no challenge here, the 0234-6 is the King of all drills and the 0300-20 and the 0302-20 are no competition what so ever, the poor trigger action and the bad handle on these 2 super high powered drills should make them some hard to control monsters,and lets not forget that they don't have the removable brush cartridge,like the 0234-6 has!!!!

Before I did my homework I was impressed with the larger number of amps the 0300-20 has, "8 amps sounded so much better then 5.5 amps, but now I know a lot better!!!

Be smart and consider the overall picture when buying anything!!

Quality is far more important then pure power alone, and let the truth be known, you could hurt yourself badly with a very powerful tool if you don't have complete control over it and use it with extreme caution.

Since the 0300-20 and the 0302-20 have such a poor side handle and a choppy moving trigger,in short that means you could never have full control over the 0300-20 and the 0302-20 and don't you ever try to use it as a screwdriver,I read a write up that claimed that they could not get the 0300-20 to rotate under 300 RPM no matter how hard they tried.... well with it's choppy/non smooth trigger action what else could you expect, and with all that power they have,you better watch out.

I am a very strong and large 300 pound "body builder" and I simply don't trust the 0300-20 and the 0302-20,and I hate the fact that Milwaukee Cut Some Corners When They Made The 0300-20 and the 0302-20!!!!

Now on the other hand, when speaking about the 0234-6,I simply and truly love this drill so much, that I don't even want to get a scratch on it! I might buy a second 0234-6 for use and put this 0234-6 in a picture frame or in my trophy case,because it is truly a mechanical work of art....a real master piece and oh man....what a prize!!!!
top_rant.title: The Milwaukee 0300-20 vs The Milwaukee 0234-6

Updated review: it's been years, and Arlo has a come a long way! I've changed my 2 star rating to a 4 star rating. Why the ding and not 5 star? These lights are fairly pricey for what they do, and the confusion on the base needed for them. They aren't managed in a single place I the app as easy as they could be... but they work well and have nice configuration options within the whole system.

I DO RECOMMEND ARLO SYSTEMS NOW.... VERY PLEASED WITH HOW THEY'VE OVERCOME ENGINEERING SHORTFALLS OF THE PAST.

-----
Original Review (2018?)
[NOTE: you will find this review on Arlo Lights, Arlo Pro 2, Arlo Base Station - as a buyer of this product you need to know it doesn't stand alone and you are getting yourself into the Arlo ecosystem. It's a long review, but could save you a world of headaches!]

You may have guessed by my headline that I am NOT an Arlo fan! I want to be, and I keep getting suckered into buying more Arlo products and services in hopes of overcoming their shortcomings, but it's pointless and one of the most frustrating things ever. I own an Arlo base station with 3 Arlo Pro cameras, 2 add-on Arlo Pro 2 cameras, and 2 Arlo Security lights, and the lightbridge (which I had to buy separately due to their increasingly deceptive marketing).
Speaking of their marketing... kudos to that team! They are AWESOME! They've created a nice looking brand and slick site... if only the product could live up to their false claims (which I'll cover in detail in this review).

HARDWARE:
OK, I'll give the hardware team credit where credit is due. I think they hardware is great! The image quality is great, the sound quality & mic sensitivity is decent. Battery life is quite awesome too although it is a non-issue as I now have all of my 5 arlos plugged in. I eventually plugged them all in in part to prevent having to rotate batteries, and also to support the CVR - continuous video recording - service that is available at a cost for the Arlo Pro 2. A service that I'll slam later in my review.

So you've got great hardware (and I am making the stretched assumption that they actually have a decent firmware version, which they tend to re-release and break working features constantly!).... but hardware alone isn't a full solution. This is where software comes in, and it's the software that drives me absolutely crazy! There seems to be absolutely NO decent QA done on releases, and things are constantly breaking or not working correctly. I had hoped that as time goes on they'd achieve some kind of stability, and given that their sales seem to be good and there are enough owners out there that it's worth investing in getting it right, they constantly miss the mark.

OK - Why the Arlo Pro 2 instead of the Arlo Pro? I had 3 Arlo Pros, but there were some features I wanted in the Arlo Pro 2 that pushed me to add one to my set to overcome the limitations of the Arlo Pro.

***ARLO PRO AND ARLO PRO 2 DO NOT WORK THROUGH WINDOWS!!!! ***

I read about this in the forums, and being a techie, I somehow thought I could be clever enough to make it work. Surely the other members weren't as persistent & determined as me. NOPE! I was just plain stupid to march on and buy into the system knowing this *could* be a limitation.

Arlo Pro - It won't work through glass because in order to detect (ie. trigger), it uses PIR (passive IR). This does NOT work through a window: period. I initially read this in reviews and Arlo official statements said that it wasn't "reliable" through a window, and you may get too many false positives or glare. I came up with clever ways to get rid of the glare (cam has to be squarely pressed on the glass, night vision disabled, and LED charged full indicator disabled). Following these steps, both Arlo Pro and Arlo Pro 2 visually work awesome through the glass of my windows. I can view "live" through the cameras (accepting the fact that the lag is about 5 seconds, which makes 2-way comms through a camera extremely frustrating and more or less useless), but I can't trigger to record!

Enter "Arlo Pro 2", and it's "pixel detection".

***WARNING: PIXEL DETECTION IS FALSE ADVERTISING!!!! THIS CAMERA (Arlo Pro 2) DOES NOT DO PIXEL DETECTION***

After several extremely frustrating support engagements (I'll comment on support later), I finally got confirmation that pixel detection (ie. activity zones) rely on PIR. That's right... PIR is the ONLY detection means.... and with pixel "detection", it filters out whether the activity was within the range of pixels that it is supposed to trigger on, then triggers if any pixels in the zone change. But none of the pixel detection software is used until a detection is triggered, which as I mentioned is via PIR only.... which DOES NOT WORK THROUGH A WINDOW!

This is why I got the Pro 2... I would be able to use it through a window! WRONG! But it's OK... I had a Plan C... the Arlo Pro 2 supports CVR - continuous Video recording. Sure, you have to pay extra for this feature ($9.99/mo for one cam, $14.99/mo for 2 cams, and you can get a 17% discount if you purchase a year upfront... about $150/yr for 2 cams). I was going to go forward this plan, but those clever marking folks got me yet again! PRE-ORDER the Arlo Security Light!

I went this route (Arlo Light) instead of going the CVR route... initially. Why? Because detection wouldn't work through the glass, even if I recorded the action with CVR, I'd have no activity markers to use to find interesting activity. And most importantly, I wouldn't be able to get any actions to trigger, such as sounding the siren, or recording on other devices, or notifying me. Boo :(

I was pleased to see the intro of the Arlo Security light, so I pre-ordered it. CAREFUL, the Lightbridge is MANDATORY, even if you have an Arlo base station. The setup was dead simple... or at least it was intended to be, unless you ran into issues, as I was lucky enough to with one of my cameras. In the app, the lightbridge is managed separately from the Arlo base. When you make rules, you have to make 2 sets... one under arlo base, the other under light bridge. Interestingly, when you create the rules, you can only choose triggering devices from the rule base you start under, but for the actions, you can use devices from either lights or arlo base. Why they haven't merged this in the UI is beyond me... that would make my life easier. But if they did it, that would be yet another software update that would take too many iterations to get right, and in the meantime would leave customers stranded with a partially dysfunctional system... but hey, that's par for the Arlo course...

OK, so I got some Lights and lightbridge. Mounted my lights OUTSIDE, and had my cameras inside. FINALLY! I had a pretty awesome system that did what I wanted. And then... my condo HOA buggered it all up and made me take down my light :( I found a workaround for one, light to keep it outside, but the other had to come down. Not Arlo's fault (for a change), but really annoying! And while I had them up, I wasn't able to plug them in. After tweaking how long to keep the light on for (10s), I found that the battery would last a couple months between changing. Nice! Again... Arlo makes great hardware in my opinion, and the light is no exception.

I had 2 window cameras... one I could leave the light up, and the other I had to find an alternative. My Plan B (C or D?) all along, and why I went Arlo Pro 2 (instead of just the original pro) is for the CVR feature. Without a way to trigger (ie. my light), what a disappointment! But at least I could get constant coverage... or could I? Maybe... the jury is still out on this one!

With CVR (and yes, my Internet connection is fast, and my signal from bridge to camera is strong.. should be since they are only 10' apart), I am still trying to figure out just how far the video lags. In the UI, it has a real slick timeline. Navigating is a decent experience on both mobile laptop browser. I like how easy it is to zoom in and out on the range, and get down to a specific time. The markers of events are clear for video movement and audio... but they aren't accurate. OK, maybe they ARE accurate, but unfortunately, on the timeline where the events show, there's no movement in the video. After a few basic tests, I was HORRIFIED at my findings!

Again, seems like even the simplest QA tests weren't performed before shipping the software, and charging customers. Here's a test: walk in front of the camera (behind a window... remember, there's no detection), and then stand there or move around. Then, open the timeline and see yourself at the time of the test. Can't see yourself? What?!?!? I couldn't!

I tested initially, and it seemed fine. Not even 24 hours later, with the camera still rolling, I thought I'd check again. Using a time event from one of my balcony cameras that is outdoors and does detect, I saw a person walk through my condo complex in the direction where the CVR of the window cam would pick them up. There was no way they couldn't show up on camera... yet I couldn't find them! There was video at that time... never missed a beat (as some reported where they had huge gaps in their video), but the person just never showed up! I checked a full 10 minutes before AND after (20 min total), and the person never showed. They I was really concerned that I was just looking at a static picture with a false-sense that it was a video feed. Like a feed-loop you see in any heist movies. What was going on?!?!?

So I walked outside and stood in front of my camera for 10 minutes. I had my phone in hand and in the timeline view, I bumped the timeline to "Live". I did not show up!!! I was horrified! So I thought it was behind... and I kept waiting and minutes crept by. So then I went into devices, and clicked the play icon to see LIVE view... and there I was! I was lagged by about 5 seconds, which is normal for these, but still... I was there! I even snagged a couple still images to put them in my library (useful to create a timeline for the support call I am dreading to make with these clowns).

So I went back to timeline, and sure enough, I could see myself under "live". And then poof! I disappeared! I saw the trees and building that are in view from the window, but not myself, even though I was 2 feet in front of the camera monopolizing most of the view. I rewinded a few seconds... I was no where to be seen! I was convinced I was looking at a static image anytime I looked at the timeline because there was no movement... but that was also what you'd see when there's no wind to move tree leaves. And then I saw a squirrel! It was a glorious little squirrel that proved my image wasn't static. But where was I?!??!

Frustrated, I returned to head back inside, hell-bent on writing a review since the last straw had finally snapped after a year of dicking around with these amateur products. And then something unbelievable showed in my timeline... it was ME!!! There I was... I could rewind and fast forward on the timeline. There was no jerkiness... the arlo didn't miss a single frame! I thought maybe I'd see a jump to where the window was emtpy and then I'd magically appear (if it had to throw out a buffer), but nope... I saw me walk up, stand there looking at my phone, occasionally give the camera the finger (for the live snapshot of course!), and then go back to my phone. Man, do I ever look funny when I am puzzled and frustrated and staring into my phone like a zombie for over 10 minutes!

So what happened? I was 45 MINUTES LATE ON THE TIME LINE!!!! WHAT??! I appears that all my footage queued up in a buffer somewhere then wrote into some incredibly slow storage in arlo's own or some cloud provider's datacenter.

So the timeline, with it's motion (green) and audio (blue) event indicators on it seems to be accurate. The time of those activities seems correct (assuming you can trigger), but the audio and video is timeshifted... but a LOT. I somehow fell behind 45 minutes over the course of 15 hours of recording. Totally unacceptable! But how can if ever catch up without sacrificing some frames? I think some discards are necessary now... just to get me caught up.

NOTE: My video setting is set to "Best Quality", which may be lossless, and may be what contributed to the buffer getting so far lagged behind.

So that's my experience with CVR so far. I had hoped it could overcome the limitations, but seems just as dumb and dysfunctional as the other falsely marketing features that don't do what they say.

And then I found their latest ploy "Arlo Smart Plans". Apparently using AI, they'll magically detect faces, pets, activity, etc and do customized alerts. Wishful thinking, and some great marketing... but seriously, they aren't capable of basic activity zone detection or normal recording, so how will they pull off this miracle? Oh ya... AI! Now if they leverage a reputable tech giant's machine learning APIs (such as google's or amazon's), then maybe they could pull it off. But if it's the same dev team that have been mangling their existing software stack, I wouldn't hold my breath.

So... that's my next course of action... I'm in this far, and sunk in a lot of $$ already, let's throw another $3/mo/cam on TOP of CVR subscription and capital outlay at the problem and see if that works. If I were smart, I'd cut my losses. There HAS to be decent cloud-based solutions out there. How's Nest? Google tends to good job...

SUPPORT:
OMG! Very quick responses! You'd think that would be a good thing... but you're assuming the responses are even remotely helpful. They are NOT. They don't even read my issues before hitting me with auto-responses, which I reply to , and they give me even more autoresponses. This team is outsourced, and the caliber is the lowest I've ever worked with in the industry. After spoon feeding them all my detailed QA work I'd done troubleshooting problems for them, and finally made it to engineering, best I would get is a response to confirm "you're correct", such as with the pixel detection debacle.
How about warranty? I had a camera that had a bad USB port. Eventually, they swapped my camera for another.... it took 10 weeks of back and forth! I'd update my case, hear nothing, create new cases referencing the others... this would go on for weeks, with me updating my tickets DAILY with "hello?!?! Is anybody there?!?! " Finally, it was going through twitter that I got my cases escalated so that someone would look up my case IDs. I thought i was in the clear and it still took months of back and forth to get a replacement camera. UGH!!!! Eventually they did. And I promptly sent back my defective camera. If I didn't send it within 14 days with their postage-paid, pre-addressed return express shipping envelope, they'd charge my credit card. I had to give them a card number as collateral before they'd send a replacement. Sure enough, they charged my card and that took a few more weeks of back and forth to sort out. They number they told me to call wouldn't pick up on the other line. And when I talked to support and gave them the phone number THEY gave me with the replacement camera, they said they'd never heard of that number or the location I shipped back to. I then found it written on their site to show it was legit, and the support person was surprised. They are completely and utterly disorganized internally! (Except marketing & biz dev groups... they clearly have their sh!t together and they've done an awesome job sneaking such a shoddy product into Costco, which usually has high product standards).
-----------------
Thanks for reading up to hear... as you can see, it's been a serious love-hate relationship with Arlo. The hardware is great, the marketing is SO slick, and the app UI is quite slick too. I figured out geo-fencing, which took 4 sets of rules (lightbridge: home & away + arlo bridge: home & away), but I am quite happy with my monitoring on the key cameras and ones that can leverage light bridge. If you have to record through glass because you have an HOA with strict rules, then save yourself the frustration.

I am giving the whole system 2 stars. If support were better, even with all the software issues, I'd bump my rating up to a 3 or 4. Remember, this is your SECURITY you are relying on... and while 99% of the events don't matter if they aren't recorded for any of a number of reasons, the only events that matter are the ones you NEED, such as a break-in, or other incident. I still don't have confidence I'll capture what I need to when it counts.

BUYER BEWARE! DON'T BE A FOOL LIKE I'VE BEEN! (or please do, because if sales are even better and Arlo has more resources to hire better QA and support, then myself and every other arlo owner will benefit - I am still holding onto hope they can get their act together, but only because I'm in it this far). SAVE YOURSELF THE FRUSTRATION!

top_rant.text:
First of all I want to say that I am a around the house handy man and not a pro but I know quality when I see it and I have had a very decent amount of experience with power tools.

I am guilty of owning a Craftsmen 3/8 inch, 1/3 horse power, 3 amp, corded drill "I got it because it was on sale for close to half price,I paid $20 for it.

When ever I tried to use it as a screwdriver with its so called variable speed trigger,it would almost always rotate to fast which would result in it stripping the screw heads.

Most times when I tried to use it for tightening nuts or drilling metal it would stall,so to make a long story short it was under powered and the variable speed control was almost uncontrollable !! Oh well you live and you learn !!!

So it was time for me to get a "high quality drill".

I now wanted a drill that would have plenty of power and have a "very controllable" variable control trigger and most of all I wanted this drill to be able to last me the rest of my life and I wanted it to be the best darn drill money could buy and at a reasonable price,now is that asking for to much ?

So I went on the net and started reading a ton of reviews,I read up on cordless drills first but I was turned off by their overall price along with the "over priced batteries they use",and lets also remember their not the most powerful drills you can buy,they simply cannot supply you with the torque a corded drill can,and when you push a cordless drill it puts a tremendous strain on the battery and in return the same battery that might have held its charge for hours using it for light duty will now lose it's charge in a matter of minutes when you use it for heavy duty use.

I have heard of some of the best batteries losing their charge within 3 minutes after using the cordless drill to do a really tough job.

I still think cordless would be nice for light duty jobs as a second drill if you can afford one!

So after reading mega reviews on the net, I was still having a little trouble trying to narrow it down to one drill,but the Milwaukee 0300-20 and the "Milwaukee 0302-20 a keyless version of the 0300-20" caught my eye with it's 8 amp motor and with the claim that it has the best power-to-weight ratio in its class.

All I could think was yum yum,MASSIVE POWER and TORQUE galore, and lets not forget that it's made by Milwaukee!!!!

Now I'm thinking I've got to have this drill no matter what, so I called a local tool store and asked if they had the 0300-20 and they said yes,so out of curiosity I asked them if they felt that the 0300-20 was the best in it's class and to my surprise he burst my bubble and told me no, and he said that the

Milwaukee 0234-6 Was The Best In It's Class !!!

Man I was so surprised at what he had said,and the man that I talked to was the owner of that shop, so I'm thinking to myself he must not know what he's talking about,I knew a little something about the 0234-6, I knew that it had a 5.5 amp motor and that it was a very old model, and I also knew that the 0300-20 was a much newer model with an "8 amp motor" and they cost around the same,so I'm thinking to myself "Duh" that sounds like a no brainer to me...old vs new, 5.5 amps vs 8 amps,come on, give me a break!

I was also thinking to myself do the math Mr. Tool shop owner! So I got off of the phone with him and called another local tool shop and talked to the boss who's name was Mike and asked him the same set of questions and low and behold he gave me pretty much the same answer which was the 0234-6 is a much better drill then the 0300-20,then Mike went on and told me that the 0234-6 is the best drill on earth bar none !!!

He said that he has had his 0234-6 for 15 years and he used it as a pro and in 15 years it never failed him once,and it never needed any repairs !

Now I'm thinking I must be in the Twilight Zone and I'm also thinking does Mike not know that the 0300-20 has 8 amps and the 0234-6 only has 5.5 amps and the 0300-20 is a much newer model.

I need to also tell you that some people think the 0300-20 and the 0302-20 are just like the 0234-6 but only with more power......WRONG !!!!

I even called Milwaukee tools and talked to a rep and he told me the 0300-20 was just an updated and more powerful version of the 0234-6...."He Was So WRONG"!!!

After thinking for awhile I decided to get the 0300-20 over the Keyless 0302-20 for these reasons...

Keyed vs. Keyless Chucks

Keyed chucks are tightened and loosened with a small hand tool, achieving a tighter and more secure lock on the drill bit than keyless chucks. They also provide greater torque for when you need extra power. On the other hand, keyless chucks require you to manually turn the chuck, making for quick and easy bit changes.

* Keyed chucks are especially advantageous when removing screws

* For those who may be prone to losing a key, a keyless chuck would be a sound selection !

So today I went to Lowes and had already decided in my mind that I was going to buy the more powerful 0300-20, which is supposed to be "the so called newer version of the 0234-6".

When I got to Lowes, their they both where side by side the 0234-6 and the all powerful 8 amp 0300-20,but wait just a minute the 0300-20 does not have the same rock solid sturdy side handle that is screwed into the metal frame of the 0234-6 so when I held the 0300-20 in my hands and put a lot of pressure on the side handle it flexed and I said to myself "Oh No" I don't like this handle and most of all I hate the way that it is attached to the drill,I think it is attached in a very tackie and not very strong manner!!!

The side handle on the 0300-20 was almost a deal breaker for me ,I could not break the handle ,but I sure did make it flex more then I think it should have!

Now on the other hand I tried to make the 0234-6 side handle flex or bend, no such luck it was rock steady , and the entire 0234-6 is built like a tank and I'm not kidding.

Next I tried out the trigger on the 0300-20 and the action of the trigger was not smooth at all, it was very choppy at best,let me tell you their is no way on earth for you to use a drill as a screwdriver if you don't have smooth trigger action.

I will go one step further and say I feel that it is potentially dangerous to try and use a super powerful drill when its side handle is not very steady and when its variable speed control trigger is choppy.

You cannot control the speed with a choppy trigger...so that was the deal breaker for me, a bad handle and a choppy trigger !!!!

Next I tried the trigger action on the 0234-6 and it was super smooth, I was truly impressed with the 0234-6 and very disappointed with the 0300-20,so I took the 0234-6 home with me.

When I tried out the 0234-6 , I was so thrilled and shocked by the super high quality of this work of art !!! The speed control when using the trigger was superb ,you can use this drill as a super screwdriver any day of the week, I could get it to spin at the slowest speed imaginable,and when I pulled the trigger in all the way this high quality drill spun with such smoothness and power that I found myself laughing out loud and thinking to myself this drill is a master piece.

Speaking of power, the 0234-6 has a whole ton of power and I can't ever imagine it stalling on me or anyone else for that matter"Just read all the write ups on it"!!!

Don't be fooled by the other drills that have 8 and 9 amps, to many of the high amp monsters have a short life span and none of them not a single one of them can hang with the 0234-6 as far as quality and durability is concerned.

So as far as the 0234-6 vs The 0300-20 or the 0302-20

their really is no challenge here, the 0234-6 is the King of all drills and the 0300-20 and the 0302-20 are no competition what so ever, the poor trigger action and the bad handle on these 2 super high powered drills should make them some hard to control monsters!!!

Before I did my homework I was impressed with the larger number of amps the 0300-20 has, "8 amps sounded so much better then 5.5 amps, but now I know a lot better!!!

Be smart and consider the overall picture when buying anything!!

Quality is far more important then pure power alone, and let the truth be known, you could hurt yourself badly with a very powerful tool if you don't have complete control over it and use it with extreme caution.

Since the 0300-20 and the 0302-20 have such a poor side handle and a choppy trigger,in short that means you could never have full control over the 0300-20 and the 0302-20,and with all that power they have, you better watch out.

I am a very strong and large 300 pound "body builder" and I simply don't trust the 0300-20 and the 0302-20 !!!!

Now on the other hand, when speaking about the 0234-6,I simply and truly love this drill so much, that I don't even want to get a scratch on it! I might buy a second 0234-6 for use and put this 0234-6 in a picture frame or in my trophy case,because it is truly a mechanical work of art ...a real master piece and oh what a prize!!!!
top_rant.title: The Milwaukee 0234-6 vs The Milwaukee 0300-20 / 0302-20

top_rant.text:
Update 02/07/2108
Keeps getting worse. They say it works with Alexa, but isn't reliable. Alexa: "Turn on Holiday Lights, Alexa Turn on Holiday Lights, Alexa Turn on Holiday Lights"!!!!!!!!!!! This is SO FRUSTRATING. Please GE Tkae this product off the market. It's totally garbage. Amazon: Kick this product off Alexa - IT DOES NOT WORK!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

Oh, almost forgot: After a brief power failure, the lights all reset to FULL BRIGHTNESS, not the previous dim preset. Amazon: can I return this junk, poorly engineered garbage?

Update 8/28/2016
Don't buy as the recent "update" on the Bluetooth devices now requires an exorbitant add-on fee to add features that were promised from the initial release of the device. GE should be prosecuted for this deceit. Seriously, buy at your own risk as every time they add features, they've required additional fees to use them. I already paid for one "premium" update and now they've changed it to a "pro" update that want to extort more money from me. They won't be around long with this business plan and you'll be stuck with unsupported devices.

Update 4/1/2016
HERE WE GO AGAIN!!! Another switch HAS FORGOTTEN the schedule it's programmed to follow. Third time this has happened. The program in the App is correct, but the switch turns on earlier than programmed. I have to delete the switch, reprogram, take another pic and add it to the app. PURE GARBAGE. ARE YOU STILL REALLY THINKING OF BUYING THIS SOFTWARE BUGGED JUNK?
I'm going to try and return all of this defective product or return every piece via warranty return. What a hassle!

UPDATE 2/8/2016
Really sorry I bought these REALLY expensive low-feature dimmers. The manufacturer called me months ago trying to sway my review (I did change it until I realized I was duped by them) and said they'd have updates and more features to the AVI-ON software, a remote web gateway, and more new products released by CES 2016 in January. It's February/2016 and STILL NO UPDATES or new products released. Either GE/JASCO Engineering can't meet their design schedules OR I was LIED TO by the company. This technology might be dying. AVOID BUYING AS IT"S APPARENTLY ALREADY OBSOLETED and NOT SUPPORTED.

Also note the glowing 5-Star review of this product (December 29, 2015) is NOT A VERIFIED AMAZON PURCHASE. Likely planted by the manufacturer.

Update 12/06/2015
I purchased my dimmers, so my reviews ARE unbiased!

Update 12/2/2015
Erratic operation has started. Didn't turn-on any lights at sunset today. Two days ago, the switches locked-up and would respond to commands at the switch. Will not dim fully now from switch - need to use app the fully dim.

NO LONGER RECOMMENDED AT THIS TIME. DO NOT BUY. SORRY I CAVED TO THE MANUFACTURER'S REQUEST TO CHANGE MY RATING.

UPDATE: 11/17/2015
I obtained a wall switched module and installed it midway between the two farthest switches (front and back of house). It's all working properly now. It's still CURRENTLY an expensive way to provide scheduled light switch control, albeit a higher tech solution than the cheaper methods. If the price was HALF of the current retail, then it would be competitive. The AVI-ON literature talks about how cheap it is to implement this Bluetooth technology, but the cost of Insteon, Z-wave, and Zigbee hve dropped (controllers too) and is more economical currently to implement a whole house lighting scheme. PLUS, the other technologies have WAY, WAY more functionality. In fact, X-10 has more functionality at a cheaper price.

Your move AVI-ON. Release more features/functions in your app to get my full recommendation! When you're not first to market, you're supposed to LEAPFROG the competition with more, NOT less. I'm sure someone in your corporate structure will read this and chuckle and think "I told them so"!

EARLIER REVIEW:
I revising my review based on calls I received from the manufacturer to try to resolve the marginal range situation in my particular house.

Problem Description:
The phone app keeps switching between the individual switch networks with no explanation of what's going on as a switch and/or app connects and disconnects in the "MESH(s)". This is a result of the limited range of the Bluetooth network, and in my case, I have one dimmer switch that's apparently too far away from the rest of the 4 dimmers which then forms its own one switch "mesh". The app makes it look like you are connected to ALL the switches and will accept and show a command on the distant switch, but in fact it is not carried out. Even if you walk towards that distant switch, it doesn't connect and carry-out the command. It just gives up and shows an error pop-up that the app couldn't connect to a network.

The software really needs to indicate which switches are in range AND connected to the mesh, not just what switches have been previously paired. I guess the software has no handshake capability to acknowledge the state of the switch?

If you locate yourself between the distant switch and the others that formed a mesh, and then start the App, it will connect with one mesh, but if you issue a command to the network mesh it didn't connect to (again, the App makes it look like it executed, but it does not) and then walk to that mesh, but away from the connected mesh, you see the app toggle with the message: "Just a Sec... We are connecting to your network" and then eventually time-out with an error message: "We can't find your network right now" and back to "Just a Sec... We are connecting to your network" but does not now connect to the in range mesh. The cycle continues... wash, rinse, repeat....

Resolution:
Looks like I'll have to buy a plug-in wall dimmer module to act as a bridge from the front to back of the house to complete a single mesh. I had to do similar actions with the X-10 systems I installed in previous houses.

The Future:
Pease add diagnostics software to the App to help root out these types of issues and at the same time, make your system more robust.
Have the App indicate which devices are connected and which aren't. Show acknowledgement of a command successfully executing.
Develop a cheap "bridge" to extend the network. Have it take no more than one receptacle and even then, see if it can "pass-through" the electrical circuit.

I bought these devices knowing I was an early adopter and hoping (actually counting on) additional devices and functionality. For example, you can't program the dimmer to daily turn onto a preset dimmer level. Your options are "on" or "off". Also, although it does have sunrise/sunset tables, you can't adjust the either to a plus or minus delta, only a fixed time. I want some of my lights to turn-on XX minutes before or after sunrise or sunset.

Conclusion:
So really what you have here is an expensive remote programmable dimmer. Yep, it has some issues that I hope aren't caused by the limitations of the underlying Bluetooth technology. However, I was encouraged by the development team reaching out to me and trying to understand and resolve the issues. Also, they gave me some insight into the features/products under development and I'm looking forward to those releases over the next year.
top_rant.title: CHANGING MY REVIEW: NO LONGER RECOMMENDED AT ALL!  PRODUCT SUPPORT APPARENTLY TERMINATED.

top_rant.text:
My Wife bought me two of the Newer 18VDC Lithium Ion Batteries, locally, for my Birthday.. But the local store was out of chargers.. I have a LOT of the Older DeWalt NiCad Batteries that have begun to wear out, even though I have several Dewalt Chargers with the "Conditioner Button" on them that discharges the NiCad Packs before recharging them (Side Note: Continuously Re-Charging NiCads that aren't "Dead" KILLS THEM FASTER... I Retired from the Military, and the Military paid for my College Degrees in Electrical Engineering, which I've been doing [often for the Military, as a consultant] for over 20 Years now.. So when I have a DeWalt NiCad Battery Pack that I know is dying, I open it up, and Test EACH of the 15 "C-Cell-Sized" NiCad Batteries from inside the "Dying" DeWalt Battery Packs, Save the GOOD Cells, and Later I replace the "Dead Cells" in ANOTHER "Dying DeWalt NiCad Battery Pack with the Good Cells From a Previous "Dead Pack"... Some of my "Restored NiCad Battery Packs" have been being used for over a decade! Black and Decker ALSO uses "15 C-Sized NiCad Cells in THEIR 18VDC Battery Packs, but their Cells just don't last as long as the DeWalt Cells.. I've been Testing MANY types of Batteries for this purpose for MANY years.. I keep Data Sheets on "Drain Times, Discharge Rates,etc... So DON'T mix Different Battery Brands IF you have it in mind to do this... and if you aren't VERY Familiar with Batteries, and BUILDING BATTERY PACKS [Which most RC Enthusiasts ARE), then DO NOT EVEN TRY IT yourself.. You're ASKING for a BAD, LOUD, DANGEROUS, experience!)...

Anyway, When I "Made the Swap" To Lithium Ion Batteries, and needed the New DeWalt Charger, I popped here to Amazon to buy a charger and found the "Lithium Ion Charger/Battery Combo Deal"... "No Brainer!!" I think I have just about EVERY "Common" Professional-Grade Tool that DeWalt Makes, so swapping to Lithium Batteries was a BIG investment... Especially since I've been slowly Swapping my "Corded" Dewalt Tools to their "Cordless" tools over the last 10 years or so (Because I REALLY cant tell a difference in "Power" between my "Cordless DeWalt Tools" and my "Corded DeWalt Tools" Other than my DeWalt "30 Amp Chop--Saw", And MAYBE my Two Professional-Grade DeWalt Side-Grinders [Yes, I have two Identical DeWalt Side Grinders because I'm Lazy, and my time is Limited... So I have a "Wire Wheel" on one of my Corded Side-Grinders, and a "Cutting/Sanding Wheel" on the other one, So I don't have to keep stopping and Changing back and forth between "Cutting Wheels" and "Wire Wheels", I just "pick up the other DeWalt Side-Grinder" ;).

I LOVE these Lithium Batteries for Several Reasons:
1. They DO NOT need to be fully DISCHARGED before Recharging them... Whether you use them for 10 minutes, or TWO HOURS, You can just slap them in the charger, and it doesn't make a BIT of different, or hurt the Lithium Batteries AT ALL.
2. I can leave them sitting in my Tool Cases without worrying about the Batteries losing 1+% of their initial Recharge Voltage per day while "Just Sitting" (That's about how much NiCads discharge while "Just Sitting"... a little over 1% Per Day).
3. The new Lithium Ion Battery Packs are MUCH SMALLER and LIGHTER! On the "Older" Dewalt Cordless Tools, they sit more "Inside" of the Battery Holder than the Larger/Heavier NiCads, but that also puts them "a Little MORE out of your way", allowing you to get your tools into those tighter spots where "a Quarter Inch of Extra Clearance makes ALL the difference". The New Lithium Batteries fit "As if MADE for the New DeWalt Cordless Tools (Because they WERE... I Know.."DUH" ;) )
4. If you're used to the Long Lasting NiCad Packs, You'll find that the Lithium packs last even LONGER... if you're replacing Older NiCad packs, you Realize that your Older NiCad Packs were dying the INSTANT you pull a trigger and the tool is STRONG and FAST! I've started setting my "Clutch" again on my Cordless Drill, because if you bind up a LARGE drill bit in a Dewalt Drill with a Lithium Battery, your arm is GOING to pay for it if you don't have the Chuck-Slip-Clutch set to "Slip at a certain point" rather than twisting your arm into a pretzel... You can get away with just setting the Clutch to "Infinity" with a NiCad Battery Back (especially an older pack), but I don't advise doing that with a Lithium Battery pack!
5. (Not really a "Love" reason, but something you need to know).. With NiCad or NiMh Battery Packs, the tools begin to "Slow down" as the battery pack begins to die... With a Lithium Battery Pack, the tool just suddenly "STOPS", and you have that "OH $}{!T Moment" where you think you've burned something up... Nope, your battery just died. Some people REALLY Don't like that... However, I don't think those people have watched a REAL "NiCad Battery Discharge Curve" AS IT'S HAPPENING... Your NiCad Battery Packs "Give you EVERYTHING THEY HAVE, for as long as they CAN, and then they SLOWLY begin Dying"... They BEGIN dying SO SLOWLY that YOU *Probably* don't realize the Battery Pack is slowing down for 20 to 40 MINUTES! DURING that 20-40 Minutes, your Job is taking longer and longer to complete.. AND "Those Holes you are drilling" (Or "Boards you are cutting) is Taking LONGER to drill/cut, AND your EXPENSIVE DRILL-BITS/CUTTING-BLADES/Etc ARE WEARING OUT Faster! MOST people only notice that their Battery Packs are 'dying' during the Last 2-5 minutes, when the Tool REALLY begins slowing down NOTICEABLY... Honestly, I'd rather the Battery Pack just give me ALL IT HAS, and then SUDDENLY STOP, rather than Slowing down SO SLOWLY that I don't notice it's happening, Which is WASTING MY VALUABLE TIME, and WEARING OUT my EXPENSIVE Bit/Blades/Etc!

Honestly, That "SUDDEN STOP" that people complain about when the Lithium Battery Packs Die, is *SAVING THEM* THOUSANDS OF DOLLARS in TIME and in "Replacing Bits/Blades/etc", over just a few years time... You QUICKLY SAVE MORE than enough Money to Buy NEW BATTERIES and NEW TOOLS! They just don't realize it!

"Lithium" technology is NOT EXACTLY at the level that it's often "advertised to be".. YET (It's getting there QUICKLY though)... HOWEVER, If you buy CHEAP Lithium Batteries, you have an explosion or fire just waiting to happen! I could show you pictures from some of my "Cheaper" Lithium battery backs for my Quad-Copter Drones and other RC equipment., that have swelled up and Burst their Cases, "Just Sitting On Their Shelves" (And I ONLY buy THE BEST "Battery Chargers and 'Conditioners'" to Recharge my Hobby Batteries that are available!)... If the "Bag" that holds the Polymerized Lithium is even SLIGHTLY punctured, or over-heated enough to cause the bag to get "a melt-through-pin-hole leak", Lithium (a METAL) comes into Contact with with OUTSIDE of "Semi-conductive Metallic Outer Cover of the 'Battery Bag", which means you have "Two Dissimilar Metals in Contact with an Acid or Salt" Which is THE definition of a battery.. But the effect of a punctured Lithium Battery bag, means you have created a SHORTED, STRONG BATTERY, which basically Equals "BOMB"! If you've ever seen the old "MRE Heater Soda Bottle Bombs", the Grey "Card-Boardy" Looking stuff INSIDE the MRE Heater's cardboard-Inner-container is "Magnesium Dust" and "Iron Dust", Mixed and Compressed together with "plain Ol Table Salt".. When you add water to the MRE Heater Bag, you've Activated "A Shorted Battery", Which gets HOT to heat your food! Cool, Huh? BUT, If you "Contain" that mixture in a bottle,, well, you probably HEARD the results! So now Imagine a MUCH MUCH STRONGER LITHIUM Battery Shorting!!! Yeah.... "BIG BADA BOOM!" Or (More frequently) a METAL FIRE that you just CAN NOT put out by conventional means!!!
So I guess I'm saying "DON'T rush to EBAY to buy 'Knock-Off' DeWalt Lithium Battery Packs"!!! DeWalt takes EXTRA CARE and uses MUCH more robust materials in their Battery Packs.. which is why they are Kind of Expensive, and why the prices never seem to drop very much.. It actually costs a LOT to build a QUALITY Lithium Battery that is SAFE! But again, that "Better Battery" is paying for itself in increased production and Saved Money from NOT wearing out Bits/Blades,etc NEARLY as fast! Just, "Don't Bet your HOME/Workshop/Vehicles/etc on a Cheap Knock-off battery pack just to save a FEW dollars!" It's NOT EVEN WORTH THE RISK, Because That Cheap Battery *WILL* Fail eventually, so it's only a matter of "If you get lucky and just get a puffed-up Battery Pack", or "You get UNLUCKY and Lose your MAJOR LIFE INVESTMENTS (And I just couldn't bring myself to MENTION "Family") in a Fire/Explosion"!

BTW, I've also been buying some new DeWalt tools I've been Wanting, in the "20 VDC MODELS... Here's YET ANOTHER place where DeWalt shows their MORAL CONVICTIONS! They COULD HAVE made FAR MORE money from Professionals who insist on QUALITY Tools, by NOT making their 18VDC Lithium Battery Packs Backward Compatible with the Older NiCad/NiMh Tools.. BUT THEY DID! So we DON'T have to buy ALL NEW TOOLS... Yes, you have to buy a new "CHARGER" for the 18VDC Lithium Packs, but that charger WILL ALSO Charge your Older NiCad Battery Packs too! DeWalt could have REALLY made more money when they Upgraded and began making 20VDC Tools/Batteries, and forced everyone to buy "All new 20VDC TOOLS" (Like MANY OTHER companies are doing!) HOWEVER, Dewalt makes an ADAPTER that lets you use their new "20VDC Lithium Battery Packs" in your "Older 18DVC DeWalt Tools" ALSO! The 18VDC Battery Packs won't work in the newer 20VDC Tools, but with a cheap Adapter, your 20VDC Lithium Battery Packs WILL work in your Older 18VDC DeWalt Tools!

I calculated *MY* Estimated savings over the next year by buying new Lithium Battery Packs... and the number SURPRISED ME... They surprised me so much, I went ahead and Bought a New DeWalt Cordless "Sawzall" (to Replace my Nearly New CORDED Dewalt Sawzall). Because, Hey, If when you NEED a good Sawzall, Ain't NOTHING ELSE gonna do the job! ;) Besides, Upgrading to the New Lithium Battery Packs will not only cause the Batteries to Pay for THEMSELVES over the next year of use, but the savings also means I basically get the Sawzall FREE, With money Left over! :)
top_rant.title: Do the Math, The Batteries Pay For Themselves

top_rant.text:
This review is over 1700 words long BUT will give you info not found in other's reviews. I hope YOU find it informative.

I have the Stainless version with glass bottle. Got it Aug 4, 2015

Please note that I USE WATER THAT IS FILTERED to start with.

The filter I use is: [[ASIN:B000SSPGHG Watts Premier High Capacity In-line Water Filter (pil-10)]] under the sink.
The spigot assembly on the kitchen sink is: [[ASIN:B0018MVYPY Watts 116023 Standard Faucet, Chrome]]

I had both of these PRIOR to considering a distiller purchase.

EASY-PEASY install for those with some mechanical aptitude. I also use the very same filter on the Ice-maker.

So far, Nov 3, 2015 (91 days later) - I've made about 90 gallons. I've cleaned the unit after every 4-6 cycles with 2 Tablespoons of Citric Acid which works GREAT. 6 cycles worth of residue HERE where I am is what 2 tablespoons of the Citric is able to remove in a single cleaning cycle and cleaning it completely in ONE cycle is how I determined the cleaning interval.
I've found Citric Acid which is what the "Distiller Cleaner" is for a great price HERE: [[ASIN:B011J1R7LI Citric Acid - 2 Lbs Food-Grade - 100% Pure Powdered Crystals - Our Earth's Secrets]]

I reviewed other comments here BEFORE I purchased, making note of one in particular which as a result of reading. I DO NOT and NEVER have let the unit boil the container dry. I use a timer that is set for 5hrs 55Min. ( YOUR time will probably be different due to line voltage which will affect Watts and so, intensity of boiling.) That leaves about 1/2 cup of water in the unit. YOU will need to experiment to find that sweet-spot for YOUR location. The thing that looks like a "Start" button is in reality a resettable "Klixon" style temperature overload and you ARE tripping it every cycle IF you are NOT using a timer. My Stainless Steel tank never looks NASTY, sure there is some scaling but it is easy to clean. I suspect this is because I pre-filter and the remaining mineral impurities have NOT been concentrated, left in the unit for many cycles and then baked dry into it. The tank is PERFECT. Pristine as the day it first arrived and not discolored or "rusting" AT ALL.

The timer I use is this one: [[ASIN:B00Y3QKUQ6 Instapark TUE-12 15 Amp Heavy-duty 7-Day On/Off Plug-in Digital Programmable Timer with 3-pin Grounded & Polarized Outlet (Single)]]
I didn't even bother to set the time on it since I'm only concerned with a "COUNT-DOWN" of 5:55. The LCD window on the timer is a bit small, but for $10 you'll not hear any further complaints.

NOW to the one concern: The propaganda they use says: "Pure water, no chemicals, no unwanted impurities!" I don't know about YOU, but NO MEANS NO in MY book. SO, IF that were true, then WHY does it come with charcoal baggies (which I DO NOT use) for the water to exit through? I'll tell you why. It REMOVES impurities from water. Contaminates bind to the charcoal due to its extreme porosity and surface area density. If it were indeed PURE coming out of the distiller, there would be NO taste, and NO need for that baggie, EVER. Obviously, that isn't what YOU will experience when you use any distiller. Depending on what is in your source water, you probably WILL have that "distilled water" taste if you do not use the charcoal baggies and I'm afraid it most likely will be unavoidable. NOT AT ALL because the unit is bad, but because of one simple fact of physics: Differing vapor-points of the various minerals and "impurities" of your supply water. Obviously some vaporize at or very close to the boiling point of water, carrying them along WITH the steam. Doe's that mean this unit sucks? NO, not at all. The VAST majority of nastiness will be removed! Just understand that if you want TASTELESS water you will most likely need to use the charcoal baggies and that WILL add to your cost per gallon. A BIG point of consideration will be just how "NASTY" the water is that you start with. It could significantly impact your cleaning frequency AND cleaning material costs!

Like others have stated in their reviews which I back, it is very well made and should last YEARS. In fact, (I) completely dismantled and re-assembled mine out of curiosity and always thinking of ways to improve things. I highly recommend that YOU, do NOT do this! - I have technical expertise and ability. I can say from first-hand observation, the build quality IS very good and it SHOULD be, given the cost. Dropping it would not be a good thing though. I think due to its sheer mass it would probably break the base material which appears to be a hard plastic similar to "bakelite" from years ago. It could also quite possibly damage the heating unit. Essentially it's a very simple, glorified electric kettle with a distiller attached at the top.

For those of you wondering if it is cost effective, here are MY REAL costs broken down for about 6 gallons:
Electricity here where I live: 9¢ per KWh OFF-PEAK
The unit uses 530 watts @ 117vAC (MEASURED)
MY cycle time is 5 hrs and 55 min which means 3.13 Kwh x 6 of electricity use = $1.69 for 35.5 Hrs total run time.
Cleaning materials: 2 tablespoons costs 31¢ 2 tablespoons = 1.03 Ounces (MEASURED)
Electricity during cleaning cycle: 2¢
6 GALLON TOTAL COST = $2.02 (a little more really since there is some water left after a cycle)
1 Gallon = 34¢ This of course, minus charcoal baggies.

For REAL WORLD comparison, A local nation-wide superstore at this time sells a gallon for 88 cents which is the CHEAPEST I could find anywhere. A difference of 54¢ Using this as a reference point, you will need to be using a LOT of distilled water to justify the expenses of purchasing, using and maintaining your own distiller. I USE about 7-9 Gallons per week. Saving about $4.32 each week over store bought. At this rate, factoring the cost of the timer, cleaning supplies AND my AMAZON bought storage containers that I purchased as a direct consequence of buying the distiller, (I) will not break even for AT LEAST 66 weeks! AFTER that it WILL be a true 54¢ per gallon savings. Perfectly acceptable LONG-TERM if you USE the water.

Bear in mind that making it yourself ALSO adds LABOR on your part. You will not simply be pulling jugs off a shelf at the store. Consider what YOUR time is worth too! I figure I spend about 1 hr and 22 mins for every six gallons or about 14 min per gallon made. This is for filling the fill jug, transferring to the distiller, from the distiller to storage, transfer from storage to dispensers, and cleaning. Is your time worth more than $2.36 per hour? THAT is what MAKING the water PAID you TIME-WISE vs. buying it while you were already AT the store. Bet THAT is a bit of a surprise eh?

To store the water I put INTO the distiller: [[ASIN:B000KA2LOS Reliance Products Desert Patrol 3 Gallon Traditional Jeep Style Rigid Water Container]] I'm NOT real happy with this container. The pour-spout gasket configuration is CRAP and leaks when I pour. It sucks. READ The review I posted there. If you get one, you WILL understand what I mean... GUARANTEED. I can modify it by maybe using a large O-ring instead and make it work, but I should not HAVE to.

To store the DISTILLED water: [[ASIN:B001QC31G6 Reliance Products Aqua-Tainer 7 Gallon Rigid Water Container]]
This container is OK. I leave the CAP with the spigot on it slightly loose until I turn it on its side to use it. That keeps the pressure OFF the silicone washer built into the cap which I hope will prolong its life by not being compressed 24/7.

Is it overpriced? Well, (I) bought one so it wasn't TOO overpriced for ME at least. Could YOU build one for the price? Doubtful.
You can see that I did some math to justify it all and I DID mull it over for several days due to the long time period before I reach break-even. If YOU are using 10+ gallons per week and already have containers, this would be a easy-to-justify purchase. Less than that and it would be wise to evaluate YOUR situation much as I did.

UPDATE: 11/11/2015 – Since writing this review. I have since bit the bullet and decided to start using the charcoal sachets since the finished water DID have a taste to it. I AM a bit worried about costs. Making this decision set me back an additional $27

[[ASIN:B00GKVNI8E 5 Lbs Bulk Air Filter Refill Coconut Shell Granular Activated Carbon Charcoal]]

[[ASIN:B000YE2OMO Regency Spice Bags for Bouquet Garnis with Drawstring Tops, set of 4]]

The unit came with 6 when I bought it. So far, I've run five gallons through the first one and the water still has NO taste whatsoever.

The NEW charcoal arrived and I'm running it through two cycles of sachet, I will not know what kind of yield I can expect. I ran 10 gallons through one of the filters that came with the distiller and it was still 100% effective. I dropped it on the floor so… Started the experiment today 12-1-15. I loaded my new material for the first time and at this moment running my first gallon.

Here is the breakdown:
( I WILL update this as soon as I have good reliable data from the distiller runs.)

Bag weight: 2.2 Grams
Charcoal weight 7.1 Grams or .25 oz ( This was the amount that filled it to what I consider optimal)

OMG THAT means I will have a yield of 320 sachet's!!! for $27! Broken down, that is 2¢ per charcoal fill!

I am ESTIMATING 15 gal/fill which will EASILY be cost effective. ( that proved more than true)

Stay tuned ;-)

One final note: Store-bought water is most likely about 3 months old sitting in those milk-jug type of containers. Making it yourself will be as "fresh" as it can get and THAT may be your primary motivation for this venture.

Hope this info helps YOU make your decisions and thanks for reading.

3/4/16 - I'm working on improving the charcoal filtration. Stay tuned.
4/26/16 - I THINK I may have found a excellent solution for DIY charcoal filtration.
I ordered [[ASIN:B009VIUI04 Keurig My K-Cup Replacement Coffee Filter Set fits B30 B40 B50 B60 B70 series]] and the plan is to elevate the distiller and rig the intake of the kuerig to the distiller outlet and TADA! I will be able to EASILY use that coconut charcoal I mentioned above! I MIGHT just need to drill out the inlet to match the exit point of the distiller! I have ALL drill sizes - Letter, fractional and Number drills. SHOULD be a piece of cake! Will know more in a week or so.

UPDATE 6/21/16 WORKING LIKE A CHAMP! I drilled it out and used hotmelt glue to attach it to the spout end. EASY-PEASEY! The set of filters I got were a 2 pack. So one has charcoal, the other doesn't. I use it in 2 configurations, With and WITHOUT charcoal. WITHOUT charcoal for production water to be used to make Colloidal Silver with my own design of generator. In that configuration, I get water that registers .8 uS/Cm which a more precise scale than a standard TDM meter does. On a TDM it would read ZERO every time. ( I USE A: Hanna PWT AND I use calibration solution - BECAUSE it is necessary for CS production.) NOW REMEMBER, it works for ME with the water supply (I) have from the tap. YOUR water may be WAY crappier. For CS generation that may require 2 or 3 runs through the distiller in which case STORE-BOUGHT MAY be a better alternative for CS production. This now concludes all testing I will doing regarding this product. It STILL is spotless inside when cleaned. It has performed absolutely flawlessly across several HUNDRED gallons of production when used as I described above.
Hope This all helps you....

UPDATE 12/5/16: STILL working perfectly, Tank is PERFECT. I've made A LOT of water with it!

UPDATE 1/15/19 STILL working perfectly. I now put RO water into it. Can Make MANY gallons with nearly NO residue.

TIDBIT:
REMEMBER: RO water is absolutely NOT the same as distilled water! DO NOT use it in your car battery! IT WILL screw it up. Distilling is the most pure you will get, AND IS THE ONLY WATER YOU SHOULD USE IN A CAR BATTERY.

UPDATE: 05/10/19 - It is still going strong and looking like new. One thing I did was to start using RO water in it. WAY less sedimentation as would be expected. Has cut my cleaning cycles to nearly never.

UPDATE: 04/05/20 (4 YEARS and 8 MONTHS) - STILL looks like new and has made hundreds of gallons.
